rationale: The signals show broad, generalist demand (Google Trends: care, diet, enclosure, morphs) and consistent high-engagement Bluesky posts about morphs and specific lines (Lilly White, Cappuccino). However, most search volume is on basic care/husbandry topics we've already published. The YouTube landscape is dominated by morph guides and Cappuccino breeding debates—many of which duplicate our existing slug "cappuccino-crested-gecko-genetics" and "super-cappuccino-viability-debate." Google Trends shows a new rising query: "crested gecko morph chart" (breakout) and "repashy crested gecko diet" (breakout), suggesting breeders are searching for concrete breeding/pairing tools rather than general education. Reddit provides zero signals, limiting community heat. The strongest actionable angle is polygenic breeding strategy for commonly-bred lines where breeders lack clear expectations—but most low-hanging fruit (extreme harlequin, axanthic strategy, soft-scale genetics) are already published. After screening against our published list, nothing clears the bar for a full blog post this week.
